policy.description: Blocking line-coverage ratchets. Update measured counts in the same reviewed change whenever scope or coverage advances; never lower a floor without an explicit, justified scope change.

tolerance.rationale: Two clean local Node 22/V8 coverage runs on 2026-07-17 passed 1244 tests, and the final clean run passed 1249 tests; all three measured the identical complete 2253-line core scope at 1932 covered lines (85.752%). Issue #122's localized/late home-row regressions exercise existing navigation and action-sheet paths, advancing 17 covered lines over the reviewed 1915/2253 baseline without changing scope. The existing one-line tolerance is unchanged and permits only the previously reproduced negligible instrumentation variance; coverage-baseline.test.js retains exact below-floor, above-baseline, scope-change, and broad-tolerance negative boundaries.

Anime filler warnings follow-up for partial-library exact-title matching and the explicit four-scenario browser matrix in issues #371 and #378. Three successful local .NET 10.0.9/Coverlet integration runs on 2026-07-19 each passed all 1777 tests over the identical complete 28438-line assembly scope; two measured the reviewed high-water of 20050 covered lines (70.504%) and one measured 20047. A separate attempt that hit the pre-existing FileLogger shutdown-timeout flake is excluded from these successful measurements. The legitimate 37-line scope increase adds conservative provider-title indexing, ambiguity rejection, and episode-title fallback for libraries that omit earlier seasons; all 37 added lines are covered. The three-line low-side tolerance is limited to the reproduced Coverlet concurrency variance and remains far below the policy maximum; coverage-baseline.test.js retains the exact scope-drift, regression, stale-baseline, and broad-tolerance negative boundaries.
